headline: Live Nation Reaches Settlement in Federal Antitrust Case
summary: The Justice Department and  Live Nation  have reached a settlement that resolves the government’s monopolization lawsuit against the company, sparing the concerts-and-ticketing giant from the threat of its business being broken up.  The deal would make it easier for other promoters to compete for business at amphitheaters and arenas that Live Nation controls, and would restrict the company’s ability to use exclusive ticketing contracts at venues.  New York Attorney General  Letitia James  said last month that she would continue the case “regardless of the path that the Department of Justice takes.”

content.title: Live Nation Reaches Settlement in Federal Antitrust Case
content.summary: The Justice Department and  Live Nation  have reached a settlement that resolves the government’s monopolization lawsuit against the company, sparing the concerts-and-ticketing giant from the threat of its business being broken up.  The deal would make it easier for other promoters to compete for business at amphitheaters and arenas that Live Nation controls, and would restrict the company’s ability to use exclusive ticketing contracts at venues.  New York Attorney General  Letitia James  said last month that she would continue the case “regardless of the path that the Department of Justice takes.”

headline: Hims partners with Novo Nordisk, Live Nation settles antitrust suit
summary: Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman takes a look at some of Monday morning's trending tickers and stories, including Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) to start selling Novo Nordisk's (NVO) GLP-1 weight-loss drugs on its platform, Live Nation (LYV) settling its antitrust lawsuit with the Department of Justice (DOJ), and cruise line stocks taking a hit from escalations in the Iran war. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Live Nation Gets to Keep Ticketmaster in Antitrust Agreement. The Stock Pops.
summary: Live Nation Entertainment  stock climbed on Monday after the company settled an antitrust lawsuit and avoided breaking off its Ticketmaster subsidiary.  The stock was up 6.8% to $166.68, on pace for its highest close since Sept. 17, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  Justice Department lawyers announced the settlement just days after the almost 2-year-old antitrust case went to trial.

content.title: Live Nation Gets to Keep Ticketmaster in Antitrust Agreement. The Stock Pops.

Justice Department lawyers announced the settlement just days after the almost 2-year-old antitrust case went to trial.
